extra.reasoning: The property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never jointly true. rec_drug_a is set true only by rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), whose condition is a 12-way AND that includes the conjunct pregnancy. rec_drug_b is set true only by rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50), whose condition is simply pregnancy. Each output has exactly one rule that can set it true, so priorities/ties/defaults are irrelevant: each fires whenever its condition holds, and defaults only apply to outputs left unset. Because the rec_drug_a condition logically entails pregnancy, any input that turns rec_drug_a true must have pregnancy true, which in turn forces rec_drug_b true. Thus rec_drug_a true implies rec_drug_b true, making joint truth unavoidable whenever a fires. Concrete counterexample: set all 12 conjuncts (pregnancy, renal_impairment, diabetes, ckd_dialysis, polypharmacy, hepatic_impairment, seizure_history, bradycardia, prior_gi_bleed, elderly, low_ejection_fraction, qt_prolongation) to true; anticoagulated and g6pd_deficiency are irrelevant (set false). Then rec_a_from_conjunction fires (rec_drug_a=true) and rec_b_from_trigger fires (rec_drug_b=true), violating mutual exclusion.
